reports.text:
**MACQUARIE GROUP – QUICK COMMERCE SECTORAL ANALYSIS**

**INDUSTRY DYNAMICS & COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE**
• **High Intensity:** Segment-wide competition is expected to remain elevated, fueled by rapid geographic expansion and diversifying product categories.
• **Amazon’s Move:** Amazon Now is scaling to **100 cities** in India, backed by a **$300 Mn** investment commitment. This signals a shift from "optional" to "core" for the global e-commerce giant in the Indian market.
• **Price Wars:** Aggressive scaling by deep-pocketed incumbents is likely to trigger renewed customer acquisition battles and heavy discounting.

**OPERATIONAL & ECONOMIC CONCERNS**
• **Dark-Store Fragility:** Rapid expansion of dark-store networks is outpacing immediate demand growth in some micro-markets, leading to underutilization.
• **Unit Economics:** Pressure on "Take Rates" and rising delivery partner incentives are straining the path to sustainable profitability.
• **Efficiency Hurdles:** Delivery cost efficiency remains the primary bottleneck as traffic congestion and labor costs rise across Tier-1 and Tier-2 cities.

**STRATEGIC EXECUTION PRESSURES**
• **Last-Mile Logistics:** The battle is shifting from "who has the product" to "who has the fastest, most reliable delivery fleet."
• **Inventory Risk:** Expanding into higher-margin non-grocery categories (Electronics, Apparel) increases inventory holding risk and returns management complexity.

**CORE INSIGHT**
• While Quick Commerce (QC) has achieved high consumer adoption, the **"Amazon Entry"** at scale shifts the game from a growth race to a capital endurance race.
• Existing players like Zepto, Blinkit, and Swiggy Instamart face a double-edged sword: they must expand to protect territory, but every new dark store temporarily dilutes their margin profile.
• Unit economics will remain under fire until the industry moves toward a higher **Average Order Value (AOV)** by including high-value non-staple goods.

**Impact: Negative (Sectoral Margin Compression)**

THE INDIA COOLING ECONOMY – STRUCTURAL THEME TRACKER

1. CONSUMER COOLING (THE RETAIL ENGINE)
• ACs & Refrigeration: Voltas, Blue Star, Havells (Lloyd), Johnson Controls-Hitachi.
• Fans & Coolers: Symphony, Crompton Greaves, V-Guard Industries.
• The Thesis: Shifting from "discretionary" to "essential" due to extreme heatwaves and urbanization; massive runway given current 7-9% AC penetration.

2. INDUSTRIAL & DATA CENTER COOLING (THE INFRA ENGINE)
• EPC & Process Cooling: Thermax, Isgec Heavy Engineering.
• Heat Exchangers: KRN Heat Exchanger (Specialist), Patels Airtemp, Shree Refrigerations.
• HVAC & Chillers: Blue Star, Voltas, Johnson Controls-Hitachi.
• Pumps: Kirloskar Brothers.
• The AI Tailwinds: Hyperscale Data Centers require 24/7 liquid cooling; thermal management now accounts for ~40% of DC energy consumption.

3. MANUFACTURING & COMPONENT BACKBONE (THE PLI LAYER)
• Contract Manufacturers (ODMs): Amber Enterprises (Critical components), PG Electroplast, EPACK Durable.
• Electronics: Dixon Technologies.
• Value Add: Moving from "screw-driver assembly" to high-value component manufacturing (motors, PCBs, heat exchangers) under PLI schemes.

4. ANCILLARY & ELECTRIFICATION (THE MULTIPLIER)
• Cables & Wires: Polycab India, KEI Industries.
• Rationale: Every cooling installation acts as a multiplier for electrification; driving demand for specialized, high-load carrying wires and circuit protection.

CORE INSIGHT
• Cooling is the "New Electricity Proxy." It sits at the intersection of Climate Change, Urban Consumption, and AI Infrastructure.
• The theme offers both immediate seasonal demand (Retail) and long-term structural capex (Industrial/Data Centers), making it a multi-decade compounding play.

Impact: Highly Positive

reports.text:
INFOBEANS TECHNOLOGIES – AI PRODUCT LAUNCH: RAI

PRODUCT OVERVIEW: RAI (RELIABILITY & ASSURANCE INTELLIGENCE)
• Positioning: An AI-native QA agent specifically designed to validate AI-generated code within CI/CD (Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment) pipelines.
• Core Purpose: To bridge the "trust gap" in software development where AI-generated code often requires extensive manual review before deployment.
• Value Proposition: Reduces manual QA cycles while ensuring high reliability for agentic-generated code.

TECHNOLOGICAL ARCHITECTURE
• Layer 1: Deterministic Core Platform: Provides a stable, predictable foundation for testing workflows.
• Layer 2: Embedded QA Domain Intelligence: Incorporates deep testing logic and industry-standard QA protocols.
• Layer 3: Multi-Agent (Agentic) Workflows: Autonomous agents that can simulate complex user behavior and system stress.
• Engine: Powered by the Qi core engine, a sophisticated pytest-based CLI for seamless automation.

ECOSYSTEM & INTEGRATION
• AI Synergy: Native compatibility with Claude.ai and Claude Code (Anthropic ecosystem).
• Workflow Tools: Deep integration with Jira, Trello, and Allure for defect tracking and reporting.
• Testing Breadth: Comprehensive coverage across UI, API, Database, and Performance layers.

BUSINESS MODEL: SERVICES-AS-SOFTWARE (SaS)
• The Pivot: Moves from traditional labor-intensive billing to a hybrid model combining high-margin software tools with managed QA expertise.
• Moat: Proprietary technology (RAI) paired with 2,000+ specialized professionals creates a high entry barrier for pure-play software competitors.

STRATEGIC RECOGNITION & STRENGTH
• Award: Named ServiceNow APAC Transformation Partner of the Year 2026, validating their high-tier enterprise execution.
• Global Reach: Operations across India, US, and Europe provide a diverse 24/7 delivery model.

CORE INSIGHT
• InfoBeans is aggressively carving out a niche in the AI Validation layer**—a segment that is becoming mandatory as enterprises scale their use of LLMs for coding.
• By aligning with the Claude ecosystem, they are positioning themselves as a specialized alternative to the broader Microsoft/GitHub Copilot testing stack.
• The "Services-as-Software" model is a smart structural play to improve revenue-per-employee and drive valuation re-rating beyond traditional IT services multiples.

**Impact: Highly Positive

KNOWLEDGE MARINE & ENGINEERING WORKS (KMEW) – GROWTH TRACKER

SHIPYARD & SHIPBUILDING PIVOT
• Investment: Investing ~₹100 Cr into a dedicated shipyard to build tugs and small-to-mid vessels.
• Revenue Target: Management targeting ₹500–700 Cr (3x FY25 revenue) from this segment within 3 years.
• Execution: Already reported ₹26.5 Cr revenue from newly operational shipbuilding activities in Q3 FY26 alone.
• Strategic Shift: Moving from "hiring" vessels to "building and owning," which is expected to structurally enhance long-term margins.

DREDGING MARKET OPPORTUNITY (THE TAM)
• Total Addressable Market (TAM): Exceeds ₹3,000 Cr annually across major and minor Indian ports.
• River Dredging Boom: As 20+ National Waterways become operational (Budget 2026 push), the river dredging market is projected to scale from ₹1,500 Cr to ₹5,000 Cr.
• Headroom: KMEW’s current dredging revenue is <₹200 Cr, leaving massive runway for market share capture.
• Competitive Edge: High entry barriers due to stringent pre-qualification norms (track record and technical seabed data).

FINANCIAL PERFORMANCE & OUTLOOK (FY26)
• FY26 Estimates: Revenue projected at ~₹260 Cr (↑ 30% YoY).
• Order Book: Robust pipeline of ₹1,500 Cr (as of March 2026) with an additional ₹3,000 Cr in active bids.
• Profitability: Forecasted PAT CAGR of 71% (FY25–28E) as high-margin dredging and shipbuilding contracts scale.
• Tax Efficiency: Successfully transitioned to the "Tonnage Tax Scheme" (AY 2026-27), which significantly lowers the effective tax rate and boosts free cash flow.

CAPITAL ALLOCATION & GOVERNANCE
• Borrowing Limit: Recently increased from ₹500 Cr to ₹1,200 Cr (March 2026) to fund the shipyard and fleet expansion.
• Cash Surplus: Deployed ~₹273 Cr from preferential issues into FDs as a "war chest" for vessel acquisition.
• Institutional Interest: Institutional stake increased by ~2% recently, reflecting confidence in the transition.

CORE INSIGHT
• KMEW is no longer just a "small-cap dredging firm"; it is becoming a "Marine Infrastructure Proxy."
• The expansion into the shipyard business creates a self-sustaining cycle—building its own fleet at cost while selling specialized vessels (like green tugs) to ports.
• The ₹3,000 Cr+ dredging opportunity in inland waterways is the most significant "alpha" generator over the next 5 years.

Impact: Highly Positive

NIPPON LIFE INDIA AMC (NAM-INDIA) – CONTRA CASE STUDY

HISTORICAL ENTRY RATIONALE (THE CONTRA VIEW)
• Valuation Moat: Entry at 16 PE during peak pessimism (fears of Zerodha/Jio entry and TER cuts).
• The "Two-Wheeler" Analogy: Identifying the sector in its 1990s-style infancy, with a multi-decade compounding runway.
• Sentiment Filter: Low webinar attendance (61 people) acted as a bullish signal of extreme under-ownership.

THE 5-POINT FUNDAMENTAL THESIS
1. Structural Tailwinds: Financialization of Indian savings ensuring 15%+ AUM growth.
2. Product Alpha: Leadership in passives and ETFs (now ~33% of their AUM).
3. Sticky Inflows: High-conviction SIP book (~₹447 Bn annualized) and "no-nonsense" fund management.
4. Asset Balance: Prudent mix between Equity and Debt, avoiding the "yield-chasing" trap.
5. Governance: Refusal to engage in toxic NFOs or low-quality AUM gathering during bull market peaks.

BUSINESS MODEL SUPERIORITY
• Uniformity: Consistent revenue streams compared to the "feast or famine" cycles of Investment Banking.
• Sustainability: Higher terminal value due to the "sticky" nature of AUM compared to the price-sensitive nature of discount brokerage.
• Profitability: Asset-light model with best-in-class dividend payouts (~91% in FY26).

OPERATIONAL MILESTONES (AS OF Q4 FY26)
• AUM Scale: Total closing AUM at ₹7.73 Trillion.
• Market Share: 8.89% (Rising for several consecutive quarters).
• Efficiency: ~58 new SIPs per minute, highlighting massive retail penetration.

CORE INSIGHT
• NAM-INDIA has transitioned from a "mispriced contra bet" to a "core compounding machine." 
• The deep research—studying 40 years of global MF history—provided the "informational edge" needed to hold through the noise of fee-compression narratives.
• At a potential 5x return, it validates the strategy that one well-researched contra bet can fund the "latency" of 3–4 other stagnant portfolio positions.

Impact: Highly Positive (Conviction Re-rating)

reports.text:
RBI FINALSES ECL NORMS: APRIL 2027 ROLLOUT FOR PROACTIVE PROVISIONING

• RBI mandates Expected Credit Loss (ECL) framework effective April 1, 2027
• Replaces reactive "incurred-loss" model with "forward-looking" credit risk recognition
• Transition impact adjusted against opening retained earnings, not through P&L
• Three-stage classification: Stage 1 (Standard), Stage 2 (High-Risk), Stage 3 (Impaired)
• Minimum provisions: 0.4% for Stage 1 and 5% for Stage 2
• ECL calculated via Probability of Default, Loss Given Default, and Exposure
• Banks must use probability-weighted estimates across multiple macroeconomic scenarios
• Transition impact spread over four years, ending March 31, 2031
• 90-day delinquency norm retained for consistent NPA identification across sectors
• Mandatory fair valuation of entire loan portfolios on the transition date

Impact: Negative to Neutral – Short-term capital hit likely; long-term structural banking stability improves

NVIDIA VS. HYPERSCALERS: THE SHIFT TO CUSTOM AI SILICON

• Nvidia's monopoly fractures as hyperscalers build in-house AI chips
• Google TPU forecast: 2.7M units vs. Nvidia's 9.4M GPUs
• Amazon-Anthropic alliance: $25B investment in 5 GW compute capacity
• Meta's MTIA chips expected to cut ownership costs by 44%
• Tesla-Samsung $16.5B deal for AI5/AI6 chips by late 2026
• Huawei Ascend 910C and CANN architecture rival Nvidia's CUDA
• OpenAI-Broadcom partnership targets custom silicon production by 2026
• Shift from general GPUs to model-specific, cost-efficient ASICs
• Major labs move toward silicon independence and compute sovereignty

Impact: Negative for Nvidia’s dominance; Positive for Hyperscalers’ margins

reports.text:
ZENSAR TECHNOLOGIES Q4 FY26 & OUTLOOK SUMMARY

FINANCIAL PERFORMANCE (Q4 FY26)
• Net Profit (PAT): ₹210.6 Cr (↑ 19.4% YoY | ↑ 5.6% QoQ) — Beat Estimates
• Revenue (USD): $158.4 Mn (↑ 1.0% YoY | ↓ 1.3% QoQ)
• EBITDA Margin: 14.7% (↓ 130 bps QoQ) — *Impacted by lower working days and large deal transition costs.*
• Dividend: Final dividend of ₹12.60 per share (630% on ₹2 FV).

ORDER BOOK & DEAL PIPELINE
• Record Order Book: $401.8 Mn (↑ 122.9% QoQ) — All-time high driven by a significant strategic large deal win.
• AI-Led Pipeline: Management noted that a substantial portion of new order bookings is now "AI-influenced."
• Vertical Trends: Banking, Financial Services, and Insurance (BFSI) remain strong; Telecom, Media, and Technology (TMT) continue to face revenue headwinds (↓ 9.7% for the full year).

AI IMPACT & WORKFORCE STRATEGY
• Hiring Outlook: Management expects muted hiring in the near term as AI-led productivity begins to ramp up.
• Fresher Impact: AI tools are compressing effort for entry-level tasks, leading to a potential structural shift in fresher intake.
• Reskilling: 85% of the workforce is now AI-certified, as the company transitions to an "AI-embedded" delivery model.

STRATEGIC VIEW ON SAAS & APPS
• SaaS Resilience: Contrary to market fears of a "wipeout," Zensar believes SaaS companies will coexist, though their delivery models will evolve.
• Product Shift: Expects a trend where rigid SaaS products are increasingly replaced by agile, AI-integrated custom applications tailored to specific enterprise outcomes.

GUIDANCE & OUTLOOK
• Q1 FY27: Management expects Q1 FY27 to be better than Q4 FY26, signaling the start of a recovery cycle.
• Margins: Aiming to sustain operating margins in the mid-teens range for FY27, despite transition costs from new large deals.
• Cash Position: Strong net cash position of $319.5 Mn, providing a buffer for potential M&A and continued high dividend payouts.

CORE INSIGHT
• Zensar is successfully pivoting into a "mid-tier large deal" player, evidenced by its record $401M order book. 
• While near-term revenue growth is modest (3.1% for FY26), the transition to an AI-native delivery model is helping protect margins and drive higher per-employee productivity.
• The aggressive dividend payout (91.5% for the year) underscores the RPG Group's confidence in the company's "cash cow" status.

Impact: Positive

**Q4 FY26 PERFORMANCE HIGHLIGHTS** • **Profitability:** PAT beat driven by strong core operating performance.

**FUTURE GUIDANCE & METRICS** • **ROA:** Guided at 1.6% – 1.7%  
• **ROE:** Guided at 13% – 14%  
• **Growth Visibility:** Operating metrics suggest the bank is entering a high-growth phase while maintaining credit discipline.  

**CORE INSIGHT** • City Union Bank has successfully navigated past asset quality hurdles and is now pivoting back to an aggressive growth stance.  
• The sharp decline in SMA levels and the 25.7% loan growth indicate that the bank's MSME-heavy engine is firing on all cylinders again.  
• With ROA approaching the 1.7% mark, the bank is trading at attractive valuations relative to its historical averages.  

**Impact: Highly Positive**

AU SMALL FINANCE BANK – Q4 FY26 CONCALL UPDATE

PROFITABILITY & ROA GUIDANCE • Target: Management aims to sustain the 1.8% ROA (Q4 exit rate) on a full-year basis for FY27.  
• Drivers: Growth in profitability will be fueled by improving operating leverage (OpEx/Assets) and stabilizing credit costs.  

CREDIT COSTS & ASSET QUALITY • FY27 Estimate: Analysts are advised to model ~90 bps for the full year.  
• Caution: Management flagged that the Q4 exit rate of 60 bps was seasonally strong and should not be used as a sustainable baseline.  
• Slippages: Q4 benefited from lower slippages and favorable day counts, which may normalize in the coming quarters.  

OPERATIONAL EFFICIENCY (OPEX) • Near-Term Target: Cost-to-Assets ratio expected to dip below 4% in the current financial year.  
• Long-Term Target: Aims for 3.5% within 3–5 years.  
• Tech Catalyst: Significant reliance on AI and automation to drive these efficiency gains.  

MARGINS & YIELDS • Cost of Funds (CoF): Management believes CoF has likely bottomed out, suggesting limited room for further interest expense reduction.  
• Yield Pressure: Potential pressure on yields due to changes in the asset mix and the absence of Q4's seasonal tailwinds.  

GROWTH & SUSTAINABILITY • Compounding: Management expects the franchise to grow at 2 to 2.5 times India's nominal GDP growth rate sustainably.  
• Universal Bank: The strategic focus remains on strengthening the foundation for a potential transition to a Universal Bank license.  

CORE INSIGHT • AU SFB is pivoting from "growth at all costs" to "profitable efficiency." The 1.8% ROA target is ambitious but backed by a clear roadmap to cut the cost-to-assets ratio via AI.  
• By guiding for 90 bps credit costs (vs the current 60 bps), management is setting a conservative floor, which provides a buffer for the stock's valuation if macro conditions fluctuate.  

Impact: Positive

TATA STEEL : ORISSA HIGH COURT QUASHES MINING DEMAND NOTICES WORTH ₹4,313 CR. ⚖️✅

• 🏛️ Orissa High Court pronounced judgement on April 20, 2026 on two writ petitions filed by Tata Steel against demand notices for alleged shortfall in chrome ore dispatch from Sukinda Chromite Block

• 💰 Demand Letter 1 (July 2025): ₹1,902.72 cr. | Demand Letter 2 (Oct 2025): ₹2,410.89 cr. — combined contingent liability of ~₹4,313 cr. now stands quashed to the extent contrary to court's directions

• ⚖️ Key ruling: Penal provisions under Rule 12A sub-rules (1A), (1B) & (1C) cannot be applied retrospectively — Mining Plan prevails over MDPA in case of inconsistency; demand notices stand quashed

🟢 Impact: Strongly Positive (Removal of a massive ~₹4,313 crore contingent liability is a significant relief for Tata Steel — landmark ruling also sets a positive precedent for the broader mining industry on applicability of dispatch shortfall penalties)

GOVT MAY TWEAK DLI SCHEME FOR SEMICONDUCTOR CHIPS AND ALLOCATE FUNDS ON A LIKE-FOR-LIKE BASIS ONLY TO CO'S OR STARTUPS THAT SUCCESSFULLY RAISE FUNDS FROM ANGEL INVESTORS OR VENTURE CAPITAL (VC) FUNDS. ( MOSCHIP TECH )

RBI ECL FRAMEWORK – FINAL DIRECTIONS & CITI ANALYSIS

IMPLEMENTATION TIMELINE
• Effective Date: April 1, 2027 (SCBs excluding SFBs and Payment Banks).
• Glide Path: A 5-year transition period (up to March 31, 2031) to absorb capital hits.
• Fair Valuation: Mandatory fair value assessment of the entire loan book on the transition date.

KEY PRUDENTIAL PROVISIONING FLOORS
• Stage 1 (Standard): - General: Aligned at 40 bps.
  - Relief: Lowered to 25 bps for Individual Home Loans and SME/Farm-related loans (CGTSME/NCGTC).
  - Risk Weight: Retained at 1% for Unsecured Retail (credit cards/personal loans).
• Stage 2 (Significant Increase in Risk): - Floor: Held at 5% for most products (despite industry lobbying).
  - Exceptions: 1.5% for Home Loans/Residential LAP/Gold Loans; 2.5% for Commercial LAP.
• Stage 3 (Credit Impaired/NPA): - Secured (Home/Gold): 10% (≤1 yr) scaling up to 100% (>4 yrs).
  - Unsecured Retail: 25% (≤1 yr) and 100% (>1 yr).

CRITICAL REGULATORY CHANGES
• Seasoning Requirement: The previously proposed 6-month "cool-off" period to move from Stage 3 back to Stage 1 has been withdrawn. This provides relief for faster asset classification recovery.
• EIR Method: Banks must adopt the Effective Interest Rate (EIR) method for income recognition by March 31, 2030.

CITI IMPACT ASSESSMENT
• Net Positive: Banks with high concentration in Home Loans (HL) benefit from the lower 25 bps Stage-1 floor and the 1.5% Stage-2 floor.
• Adverse: Banks with high 30–90 DPD (Days Past Due) exposure in Unsecured Retail, MFI, and Vehicle Finance will face higher provisioning due to the 5% Stage-2 floor.
• PSB Pressure: Public Sector Banks (PSBs) may face higher capital pressure due to older vintage Stage-3 accounts and historical staging data gaps.

CORE INSIGHT
• The ECL framework forces banks to recognize stress earlier. The retention of a 5% floor for Stage-2 is a conservative stance by the RBI to prevent "under-provisioning" in volatile retail segments.
• Withdrawal of the seasoning requirement is a major operational win for banks, allowing them to reclassify cured accounts immediately.

Impact: Positive for Large Private Banks (HL focus) | Neutral-Negative for MFIs/PSBs

IZMO LTD (IZMO MICROSYSTEMS) – SILICON PHOTONICS MISSION

THE ANNOUNCEMENT (APRIL 27, 2026)
• Official Partner: izmo Microsystems (subsidiary of Izmo Ltd) recognized as the "Photonic IC Packaging Partner" for the National Silicon Photonics Mission.
• Collaboration: Led by the Center of Excellence for Compound Photonics and Photonic Integrated Circuits Systems (CoE-CPPICS) at IIT Madras.
• Governance: Formally disclosed via SEBI Regulation 30 filings to BSE and NSE.

THE TECHNOLOGICAL STACK (WHAT'S READY)
• Design PDK: Indigenously developed Process Design Kit (PDK) with over 50 verified components (Launch: April 24, 2026).
• Test Engine: Universal Programmable Photonic IC (PPIC) automated test platform ready for module characterization.
• Foundry Partner: SilTerra Malaysia (CMOS-compatible silicon photonics foundry).
• Packaging (The Izmo Link): Specialized sub-micron precision packaging required to convert raw photonic chips into functional, deployable systems.

STRATEGIC ROLE OF IZMO MICROSYSTEMS
• Function: Handles heterogeneous integration (combining light-based and electronic components) and 3D System-in-Package (SiP).
• Capability: Precision active alignment of fiber arrays, waveguides, and laser sources—a critical bottleneck in commercializing Silicon Photonics.
• Upcoming Milestones: Multi-Project Wafer (MPW) fabrication runs starting Q3 FY26, including comprehensive testing and module characterization.

MARKET APPLICATIONS & TAM
• AI & HPC: Hardware acceleration for deep learning via co-packaged optics.
• Data Centers: Terabit-scale optical communication to reduce latency and power.
• Quantum Computing: Secure photonic networking and quantum-regime sensing.
• Telecom: Next-gen 5G/6G low-loss, high-density interconnects.

CORE INSIGHT
• While the market typically chases "Fabs," Izmo has captured the "Packaging" layer—a high-margin, low-competition segment essential for technology sovereignty.
• Without Izmo’s sub-micron packaging, the IIT Madras designs remain theoretical. This makes Izmo a "toll-gate" partner in India's deep-tech semiconductor stack.
• This transition from a "marketing solutions" company to a "semiconductor packaging" leader represents a structural shift in Izmo's business profile.

Impact: Highly Positive

reports.text:
Nobody knows anything!

It is often repeated like gospel truth that when promoters are buying, you should buy. When insiders are buying, you should buy. When companies issue bonus shares, it signals confidence from the management team.

But reality is far more complicated.

Just look at the buybacks by three leading IT firms over the last few years. Together, they bought back shares worth nearly ₹1,11,500 crores at various price levels.

At current prices, with hindsight, these companies could have saved almost ₹32,000 crores.

So were those buybacks a signal of future returns? Clearly, not necessarily.

This shows that interpreting promoter buying, insider activity, bonus issues, or buybacks as guaranteed indicators of future performance is often just hindsight dressed as wisdom.

What really matters in stocks are the following:

Sales growth
Conversion of sales into profits
Conversion of profits into free cash flow
Protection of minority shareholders’ rights

Among all of these, the most critical is revenue growth.

Because without growth in the underlying business, everything else is just an exercise in filling blank spaces in annual reports.

The rest may help, but growth is the foundation.

reports.text:
CITI on Banks

RBI issued final directions to transition to ECL framework for SCB (except SFB, payment banks) wef April 1, 2027.

Key changes:

[1] Prudential Provisioning Floors Retained: Stage-1 provisioning aligned at 40bps (standard asset equivalent); revised lower to 25bps for individual home loans and CGTSME/NCGTC-guaranteed loans (in line with SME/farm loans); retained at 1% for unsecured retail. Stage-2 floor held at 5% for most products despite industry representations; 1.5% for HL/residential LAP/GL; raised to 2.5% for commercial LAP. Stage-3 floors for home/gold loans: 10%/20%/30%/40%/100% (≤1/2/3/4/>4 years); unsecured retail: 25%/100% (≤1yr/>1yr).

[2] Five-Year Glide Path (through 31-March-2031) to cushion transitional impact.
[3] Fair valuation of the entire loan portfolio mandated at transition date.

[4] Six-month Stage-2 seasoning requirement for Stage-3 accounts prior to Stage-1 reclassification — withdrawn…

Impact: Net positive for banks with higher HL exposure; adverse for those with elevated 30–90 DPD buckets (unsecured loans, MFI, vehicle finance) and PSBs).

ANONDITA MEDICARE – WEST BENGAL RETAIL EXPANSION

DISTRIBUTION SCALE-UP • Total New Touchpoints: 1,700+ Retail Outlets  
• District-wise Breakdown: • Cooch Behar: 714 touchpoints  
  • Asansol: 616 touchpoints  
  • Hooghly: 411 touchpoints  

STRATEGIC EXECUTION • Market Penetration: Simultaneous expansion across Urban, Semi-urban, and Rural tiers in West Bengal.  
• Visibility: Targeted increase in shelf-space and product availability to capture rising healthcare/FMCG demand.  
• Last-Mile Focus: Strengthening the distribution "pipe" to ensure consistent product flow in historically underserved districts.  

GROWTH ROADMAP • Domestic Strategy: Scaling from regional dominance toward a Pan-India distribution network.  
• Global Ambition: Establishing a robust domestic foundation to eventually fuel international market entry.  
• Sustainable Growth: Focus on deep-rooted distribution rather than just high-level marketing to drive long-term volumes.  

CORE INSIGHT • For a healthcare/FMCG player like Anondita, "Distribution is Destiny." Reaching 1,700+ outlets in just three districts of West Bengal signals an aggressive localized "cluster-bomb" strategy.  
• This granular reach (especially in Cooch Behar and Hooghly) creates a high entry barrier for competitors and ensures better working capital cycles through a wider retailer base.  

Impact: Positive

WEBSOL ENERGY SYSTEM Q4 FY26 RESULTS

FINANCIAL PERFORMANCE (Q4 FY26) • Revenue: ₹401 Cr (↑ 132.1% YoY | ↑ 53.8% QoQ)  
• EBITDA: ₹146 Cr (↑ 86.4% YoY | ↑ 37.4% QoQ)  
• EBITDA Margin: 36.4% (vs 45.4% YoY | 40.8% QoQ)  
• PBT: ₹125 Cr (↑ 89.2% YoY)  
• PAT: ₹125 Cr (↑ 157.9% YoY | ↑ 91.6% QoQ)  
• EPS: ₹2.80 (↑ 140.4% YoY)

FULL YEAR PERFORMANCE (FY26) • Revenue: ₹1,049 Cr (↑ 82.4% YoY)  
• EBITDA: ₹429 Cr (↑ 69.6% YoY)  
• EBITDA Margin: 40.8% (vs 43.9% YoY)  
• PAT: ₹303 Cr (↑ 95.8% YoY)  
• EPS: ₹7.00 (↑ 90.4% YoY)

CAPACITY & OPERATIONAL UPDATES • Technology Upgrade: Converting Mono PERC line to Topcon technology; Overall capacity to reach 1.35 GW.  
• Utilization: Cell Line-2 ramped successfully; Cell utilization at >90%, Module utilization at ~74%.  
• Order Book: Strong visibility with ₹1,161 Cr in unexecuted orders.  
• Balance Sheet: Turned Net Cash Positive with ₹34 Cr net cash (₹152 Cr cash vs ₹118 Cr debt).  

STRATEGIC ROADMAP & OUTLOOK • Future Goal: Build towards a 2 GW integrated cell + module facility.  
• Backward Integration: Management focusing on internalizing more of the value chain to protect margins.  
• Sector Tailwinds: Benefiting from ALMM policies and PLI schemes driving demand for high-efficiency domestic solar products.  

CORE INSIGHT • Websol has successfully transitioned from a turnaround story to a high-execution growth phase, evidenced by record revenue and turning net cash positive.  
• While margins saw a slight YoY contraction (likely due to tech-upgrade transition costs), the 157% PAT growth signals massive operating leverage.  
• The shift to Topcon technology is a crucial competitive move to maintain relevance as the industry moves away from older Mono PERC standards.  

Impact: Highly Positive

RETAIL & OPERATIONAL METRICS (Q4) • Retail Consumption: ₹4,261 Cr (↑ 31% YoY)  
• Retail EBITDA: ₹580 Cr (↑ 19% YoY)  
• Leverage: Net Debt/EBITDA improved to 1.19x (vs 1.23x YoY)  
• Liquidity: Strong position with ₹2,004 Cr in cash/equivalents  

GROWTH PIPELINE (ROADMAP TO FY30) • Retail: Targeting >18 Mn sq. ft. (vs 11.5 Mn sq. ft. current)  
• Offices: Targeting 9 Mn sq. ft. (vs 5 Mn sq. ft. current)  
• Hospitality: Targeting 2,188 keys (vs 588 keys current)  
• Residential: 7 Mn sq. ft. development pipeline  

HISTORICAL TRACK RECORD • EBITDA CAGR: 26% over the last 20 years (FY06–FY26).  
• Scale: Grown from ₹26 Cr EBITDA in FY06 to ₹2,637 Cr in FY26.

CORE INSIGHT • Phoenix Mills continues to be the "gold standard" for playing the Indian retail consumption story, with massive operating leverage visible in the 520 bps margin expansion.  
• The aggressive FY30 roadmap across hospitality (4x capacity) and retail (60% expansion) provides high visibility for sustained compounding.  
• Strong balance sheet (Net Debt/EBITDA at 1.19x) allows for self-funded growth without equity dilution.

Impact: Highly Positive

NDTV PROFIT EARNINGS WATCHLIST: Q4 FY26

POSITIVE MOMENTUM

• COAL INDIA: PAT ↑ 11% to ₹10,839 Cr. Revenue ↑ 6% to ₹46,490 Cr. Driven by higher FSA realizations and e-auction volumes. Final dividend of ₹5.25/share declared.
• ADANI TOTAL GAS: Revenue ↑ 16% to ₹1,696 Cr. Volumes ↑ 13% YoY despite West Asia geopolitical disruptions. Calibration in passing cost hikes supported volume growth.
• PIRAMAL FINANCE: PAT ↑ 9x to ₹603 Cr (aided by exceptional gains). Total Income ↑ 56.5% to ₹4,783 Cr. Significant turnaround with strong recovery momentum.
• AU SMALL FINANCE: PAT ↑ 65% to ₹832 Cr. GNPA improved to 2.03%. ROA expanded to 1.8%. Strategic shift to secured assets driving profitability.
• CITY UNION BANK: PAT ↑ 25% to ₹360 Cr. GNPA improved to 1.91%. Announced a 1:3 bonus issue and ₹2 dividend/share. Margins at multi-quarter highs.
• PHOENIX MILLS: Strong retail consumption growth continues to drive rental income and occupancy across premium mall assets.
• WEBSOL ENERGY: PAT surges 3x YoY. Revenue consensus at ₹420–480 Cr. Benefiting from massive demand in the solar cell/wafer sector and PLI momentum.
• PRATAAP SNACKS: Turned profitable with PAT of ₹1.1 Cr (vs ₹11.9 Cr loss). EBITDA margin expanded to 4.9% vs 1.2% YoY.
• ASTEC LIFESCIENCES: Net loss narrowed to ₹7.8 Cr (vs ₹16.1 Cr loss YoY). Revenue ↑ 32.7% to ₹159 Cr. EBITDA margins improved to 5.76%.
• ROSSARI BIOTECH: Net Profit ↑ 33% YoY. Strong volume growth in Home, Personal Care, and Performance Chemicals (HPPC) segment.

NEGATIVE MOMENTUM

• JINDAL SAW: PAT ↓ 52% to ₹139.4 Cr. Revenue ↓ 8% to ₹4,634 Cr. EBITDA margins compressed sharply to 10.4% (vs 14.6% YoY) due to higher input costs and lower realizations.

CORE INSIGHT
• Financials (AU SFB, TMB, CUB) and Asset Managers (Nippon) show dominant growth, while manufacturing turnarounds (Prataap, Websol) are gaining traction. 
• Industrial/Pipes (Jindal Saw) face near-term margin pressure from global macro headwinds.

Impact: Mixed to Positive

reports.text:
APOLLO HOSPITALS | NCLT CHENNAI GRANTS KEY DATES FOR COMPOSITE SCHEME MEETINGS 🏥⚖️📋

• ⚖️ NCLT Chennai Division Bench-II passes order dated April 15, 2026 — certified copy received by Apollo Hospitals on April 27, 2026

• 📅 Revised meeting schedule approved by NCLT for Composite Scheme of Arrangement involving Apollo Hospitals Enterprise Ltd, Apollo Healthco Ltd, Keimed Pvt Ltd & Apollo Healthtech Ltd

• 🗓️ Key meeting dates set: Secured Creditors of Company 1 — May 27, 2026 (10:00 AM) | Equity Shareholders of Company 1 — May 27, 2026 (2:30 PM) | Unsecured Creditors — May 27, 2026 (11:00 AM) & May 26, 2026

• ✅ 90.96% of Secured Creditors have already given consent — strong creditor support for the scheme

• 📋 Application for dispensation of Secured Creditors meeting dismissed — NCLT ordered meeting to proceed as per schedule

🟢 Impact: Positive (Scheme progressing as planned with NCLT granting revised meeting dates. 90.96% creditor consent signals overwhelming support for the restructuring. May 2026 meetings are a critical milestone towards final NCLT sanction — Apollo Healthco demerger & restructuring remains on track)

SIGMA ADVANCED SYSTEMS: Bags ₹3,800 Cr (≈£300 Mn) 7-year aerospace systems contract from Rolls-Royce.

📍 NEW PROJECTS

📍 FUNDRAISING

JAYASWAL NECO: Raises ₹200 Cr via preferential warrant issue to promoter-linked entity.

MONARCH NETWORTH CAPITAL: Raises ₹100 Cr for PMS fund, targeting ₹500 Cr AUM.

📍 Q2 / BUSINESS UPDATE

L&T FINANCE: Reports strong Q4 with 62% YoY disbursement growth and improving margins.

ULTRATECH CEMENT: Reports strong volume growth and cost control; green energy mix rises to 43%.

📍 INCORPORATE / CORPORATE STRUCTURE

📍 IT NEWS

📍 TAX ORDER

📍 ADDITIONS

📍 OTHERS

SAMVARDHANA MOTHERSON : ACQUIRES 51% STAKE IN NISSIN ADVANCED COATING FOR ₹9.23 CR. 🏭🤝

• 🎯 Board approved acquisition of 51% equity stake in Nissin Advanced Coating Indo Co. Pvt. Ltd. ("Nissin India") from Nissin Electric Co. Ltd., Japan — for ₹9.23 crore cash consideration

• 🔬 Nissin India is engaged in thin film coating services & PVD (Physical Vapor Deposition) systems for tools, molds, dies, automotive parts & decorative goods — FY26 Revenue ₹30.4 cr., EBITDA ₹10.3 cr. (healthy ~34% margin)

• 📈 Post-acquisition, SAMIL will hold 100% of Nissin India (51% direct + 49% via subsidiary SMISL) — Nissin India becomes indirect wholly owned subsidiary; transaction expected to close by end of Q1 FY27

• 📊 Nissin India's revenue has grown steadily: FY24 ₹26.7 cr. → FY25 ₹27.8 cr. → FY26 ₹30.4 cr.

🟢 Impact: Positive (Full consolidation of a profitable coating technology business at modest cost — PVD coating is a high-value, niche capability that complements Motherson's auto components portfolio)

INFO EDGE (INDIA) : SUBSIDIARY INVESTMENT — 5 CR INFUSED INTO STARTUP INVESTMENTS (HOLDING) LTD 💼🔁⚪

• 💰 Investment size: Info Edge to invest ~5 cr in Startup Investments (Holding) Limited ("SIHL") — its 100% wholly-owned subsidiary — via cash consideration

• 📄 Instrument: 2,33,535 Compulsorily Convertible Debentures (CCDs) at issue price of 214.10/- each (including premium of 114.10/- per CCD) — SIHL remains wholly-owned subsidiary post transaction

• 🏢 About SIHL: Incorporated March 4, 2015 — engaged in direct/indirect investments in tech companies and Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs); Turnover: Nil for last 3 years; Networth: (209.50) cr as of Mar'25

• 🎯 Purpose: Funds will enable SIHL to explore new investment opportunities including AIF contributions and general corporate purposes

• 🔗 Related party: Transaction qualifies as related party — done at arm's length; no promoter/promoter group interest involved

• ⏳ Timeline: To be completed within 30 days of approval (approved April 27, 2026)

⚪ Impact: Neutral — This is a routine intra-group capital infusion of a small size (5 cr) into Info Edge's investment holding subsidiary. No material financial impact expected. SIHL acts as a vehicle for Info Edge's startup/AIF investment strategy — consistent with its portfolio investing approach (Zomato, PB Fintech, etc.). No red flags.

GOLDMAN SACHS OIL RESEARCH | BRENT TARGET RAISED TO $90 — NET UPSIDE RISKS PERSIST 🛢️📈⚠️

• 🎯 Price upgrade: 2026Q4 Brent/WTI forecast raised to $90/$83 (from $80/$75) — driven by lower Persian Gulf production & slower recovery timeline

• 🌊 Record inventory draw: Persian Gulf production losses of 14.5mb/d driving global oil inventory draws at record 11-12mb/d pace in April — market swings from 1.8mb/d surplus in 2025 to 9.6mb/d deficit in 2026Q2

• ⏳ Recovery timeline: Gulf exports normalisation now expected by end-June (vs mid-May earlier) — 70%/90% production recovery assumed by July/December; 1,830mb of cumulative production losses estimated

• 📉 Softer demand offset: Global oil demand expected to fall 1.7mb/d YoY in Q2 2026 due to higher refined product prices — but extreme inventory draws not sustainable

• 🌍 Supply outside Mideast: Russia (+0.4mb/d) & US (+0.3mb/d) partially offsetting Gulf losses

• ⚡ Three price scenarios for Brent Q4 2026: Benign ~$80 | Adverse ~$100 | Severely adverse ~$120

🔴 Impact for India: Negative (Higher crude at $90+ is a significant headwind for India — impacts OMC margins, widens current account deficit, adds inflationary pressure on fuel, diesel & freight costs. Cement, logistics & petrochemical companies face cost pressure. Watch oil marketing companies & aviation sector)

IMFA (INDIAN METALS & FERRO ALLOYS) – ANALYSIS

CORE BUSINESS MOAT • Integration: One of the few fully integrated ferrochrome players globally.  
• Captive Assets: Owns captive mines and power plants, ensuring total control over production costs.  
• Pricing Power: High operating leverage allows the company to capture maximum gains from surging global ferrochrome prices.  

CAPACITY EXPANSION & SCALE • Recent Acquisition: 150 KTPA ferrochrome plant acquired from Tata Steel (Nov 2025) is now fully operational.  
• Greenfield Capex: 100 KTPA new capacity coming online in FY27.  
• Global Ranking: Total capacity set to cross 500 KTPA (from 284 KTPA), making it the 6th largest producer globally.  

GLOBAL MACRO DYNAMICS • Geopolitical Edge: Chinese competitors are struggling with high shipping costs and supply disruptions due to the Iran conflict.  
• Supply Chain: Competitors rely on South African ore; IMFA’s domestic captive mines provide a significant structural advantage.  

TECHNICAL & FUNDAMENTAL OUTLOOK • Technical Strength: Stock has recently broken out to a new 52-week high with strong momentum.  
• Fundamentals: Solid combination of capacity growth and margin expansion driven by commodity tailwinds.  

CORE INSIGHT • IMFA is entering a super-cycle of growth, where massive capacity doubling is perfectly timed with global supply constraints.  
• Captive power and ore make it one of the lowest-cost producers globally, ensuring high margins even if prices stabilize.  

Impact: Positive

MAHINDRA HOLIDAYS Q4 FY26 RESULTS

FINANCIAL PERFORMANCE (CONSOLIDATED)
• Revenue: ₹820.29 Cr (↑ 5.3% YoY | ↑ 9.0% QoQ)
• EBITDA: ₹197.23 Cr (↓ 3.5% YoY | ↑ 36.8% QoQ)
• EBITDA Margin: 24.0% (vs 26.2% YoY | 19.2% QoQ)
• Net Profit (PAT): ₹41.56 Cr (↓ 43.1% YoY | ↑ 1,763.7% QoQ)
  *> Note: PAT decline YoY was primarily driven by a deferred tax charge of ₹13.35 Cr vs a credit of ₹18.84 Cr in the base year.*

OPERATIONAL HIGHLIGHTS
• Member Base: Cumulative members reached 3,03,906
• Membership Upgrades: ₹93 Cr (↑ 33% YoY) — strong internal monetization
• Average Unit Realization (AUR): ₹14.1 Lakhs (↑ 83% YoY) including upgrades
• Resort Revenue: ₹120 Cr (↑ 11% YoY)
• Occupancy: 82% (Maintained on a significantly higher inventory base)

INVENTORY & NETWORK EXPANSION
• Key Addition: Expanded by 213 keys to a total of 6,228 keys
• New Managed Resorts: 3 additions (Dapoli - Maharashtra, North Goa, Chikkamagaluru - Karnataka)
• Existing Resorts: Expansions successfully completed at three existing locations

CORE INSIGHT
• Top-line growth remains steady with a significant jump in unit realizations (AUR), indicating premiumization and successful member upgrades.
• While reported PAT was impacted by tax accounting reversals, the underlying operational strength is visible through high occupancy levels (82%) despite rapid inventory expansion.
• Expansion into high-demand leisure circuits like Goa and Chikkamagaluru bolsters the network effect for the member base.

Impact: Neutral to Positive (Operational strength masked by one-time tax adjustments)

GLOBAL HEALTH (MEDANTA) – INDORE ONCOLOGY ACQUISITION

TRANSACTION OVERVIEW • Target: Asian Institute of Oncology (AIOPL)  
• Asset: 79-bed super-specialty cancer hospital in Indore  
• Deal Value: ₹30 Cr (Cash deal)  
• Funding: Mix of internal accruals and debt  
• Expected Completion: Before September 30, 2026  

STRATEGIC ADJACENCY • Proximity: Located 500 meters from the existing Medanta Indore facility.  
• Synergy: Integration allows for shared operational costs and seamless patient transfers.  
• Market Gap: Specifically targets the oncology segment, strengthening Medanta's tertiary and quaternary care portfolio in the region.  

NETWORK CAPACITY EXPANSION • New Bed Count: Total network capacity increases to 3,658 beds (from 3,579).  
• Geographic Footprint: Continued expansion across Gurugram, Indore, Ranchi, Lucknow, Patna, and Noida.  

FINANCIAL & OPERATIONAL UPSIDE • Capital Efficiency: At ~₹38 Lakhs per bed, this is a highly cost-effective acquisition compared to greenfield costs.  
• Margin Profile: Oncology is a high-margin specialty; proximity to the main hospital ensures a faster ramp-up and higher ROIC.  

CORE INSIGHT • This "bolt-on" acquisition strategy enhances specialty depth in an existing cluster.  
• It leverages the "Medanta" brand in Indore to capture high-intensity oncology cases without the long gestation period of a new build.  

Impact: Positive

MAHINDRA HOLIDAYS ACQUIRES 50-ACRE CHIKMAGALUR COFFEE ESTATE FOR ₹37.5 CR 🌿☕🏖️

• 🌱 100% stake acquisition in Aditatva Estates Pvt Ltd — engaged in coffee plantation business on ~50 acre land parcel in Chikmagalur, Karnataka; to become wholly owned subsidiary

• 💰 Acquisition cost: ₹37.5 Crore (cash consideration) — no related party interest; expected to close by July 31, 2026

• 🏨 Strategic intent: Land to be utilized for development of a leisure resort — classic asset-light land banking play in a premium tourism destination

• 📈 Aditatva's revenue growth: FY23 ₹37L → FY24 ₹81L → FY25 ₹82L — small but growing plantation business; land value is the real asset here

• 📍 Chikmagalur is one of India's most sought-after leisure & wellness tourism destinations — strong demand tailwinds for resort development

🟢 Impact: Positive (Smart strategic land acquisition in a high-demand leisure destination at a reasonable price. Club Mahindra expanding its resort pipeline with unique coffee estate experience — differentiated product vs standard resorts. Chikmagalur resort could command premium pricing and attract high-value members)

Typical HVDC Capex Cost Break Up

Biggest cost = equipment & infrastructure: Civil works (~20%), valves (~14%), and converter transformers (~16%) together ta
ke the largest share → these are core physical components of HVDC projects.

Mid-level costs (~10% each): Engineering, other equipment, and AC filters each contribute around 10% → these support system design and power quality.

Smaller but essential costs: Control systems (~7%), erection & commissioning (~8%), and freight/insurance (~5%) → needed for setup, operation, and logistics.

INDIA PEAK ELECTRICITY DEMAND – 256.1 GW (25 APR 2026)

• New all-time peak: ~256.1 GW on 25 Apr 2026 (previous ~250 GW hit a day earlier)  
• Driven by an intense heatwave — rapid spike signals rising summer stress on grid and outdoor workers

Demand Outlook
• FY26 peak demand estimate: ~270 GW  
• Structural drivers: urbanization, appliance (AC) penetration, industrial recovery

Cooling penetration supercycle
• AC penetration still low (~10–12%) — heatwaves accelerate adoption

Grid & Capex Implications
• Transmission/distribution bottlenecks exposed during peaks → higher T&D capex, faster approvals  
• Positive for power equipment, EPC, transmission companies

Renewables & Storage
• Evening peaks when solar wanes increase urgency for battery storage and hybrid models

Risks & Watchpoints
• Demand volatility, discom stress, continued coal dependency

Impact: Positive for power capex and renewables/storage demand — near-term grid stress but supports long-term structural electricity investment thesis

• Q3 margin expansion: +389 bps; Q4 moderation: +102 bps — earlier efficiency gains now normalizing  
• Lower input costs (milk, edible oil) aided margins; some players saw ~+60 bps operating margin improvement  
• Risk: reversal in commodity cycle could quickly compress margins and earnings  
• Sector divergence widening:
  - Winners: Staples (Nestlé, HUL, Trent) — volume + premiumization + execution; select industrials with operating leverage  
  - Laggards: IT Services — weak demand, cautious guidance; Banks — mixed results (ICICI steady but not broad-based)
• Market behavior: becoming stock-specific rather than sector-wide bullish

What’s concerning:
• Fragile demand
• Earnings quality questions (margin-driven vs volume-driven)
• Potential guidance disappointments

Investor takeaway:
• Short term: earnings-supportive, market may hold up  
• Medium term: without a demand revival, rally lacks durability — watch commodity trends and guidance closely
Preferential equity listings have touched their highest number in over a quarter century, It Indicates companies are hungry for expansion .

Capital 360 ONE on Welspun Corp

WLCO is the leading player in the US HSAW pipe market, commanding an estimated ~30–35% market share, supported by its local manufacturing presence amid ongoing import tariffs. Hindustan Zinc — Systematix Snapshot

• Rating: **BUY**  
• TP: **Rs 724**; CMP: **Rs 589**; **22%** upside  

• Valuation: SOTP; zinc/lead 9x EV/EBITDA, silver 12x  
• 4QFY26 Revenue: **Rs 126.9bn** (+43.8% YoY)  

• 4QFY26 EBITDA: **Rs 77.1bn**; margin **56.9%**  

• 4QFY26 PAT: **Rs 50bn** (+68% YoY)  

• FY26 Revenue/EBITDA/PAT: **408/220.6/138.3 bn**  
• Zinc CoP ex‑royalty: **USD 903/t** (4Q), FY26 **USD 959/t**  

• Silver price avg: **USD 84/oz**; silver rev **Rs 40.3bn** (4Q)  
• Volumes: mined **315kt** (4Q); zinc metal **227kt** (4Q)  

• Guidance FY27: mined **1,150kt**, refined **1,100kt**, Ag **680t**  

• FY27 capex: **USD 0.5–0.6bn**; FY26 capex **Rs 36bn**  
• Dividend yield guided ~**5.1%** (FY27–28)  

• Key catalysts: silver tailwinds, CoP savings, smelter ramps  

• Key risks: silver price reversal; project execution delays; geopolitics

CIE Automotive India — Mirae Asset / Sharekhan Highlights

• Rating: Positive; Target **Rs 552** (~**21%** upside)  
• CMP referenced: **Rs 457**  

• Valuation: **16.3x/14.8x/13.6x** CY26/27/28 EPS  
• Market cap: **~Rs 17,300 crore**; 52W **526 / 381**  

• Q1CY26 revenue: **Rs 2,612 cr** (+14.9% YoY)  
• Q1CY26 EBITDA: **Rs 401.9 cr**; margin **15.4%**  
• Q1CY26 PAT: **Rs 248.1 cr**; EPS **6.5**  

• India revenue: **Rs 1,663.5 cr**; Europe: **Rs 948.5 cr**  
• Europe aided by ~**17%** forex benefit  

• CY26 capex plan: **Rs 400–500 cr** (Rs 100 cr spent)  

• Management aims to outperform industry CY26  
• Europe restructuring largely complete; margins normalizing  
• Strategic upside: China OEM localisation; Europe/US recovery  

• Key risks: geopolitical cost pressure; export demand slowdown  

• Shareholding: Promoters **65.7%**; FII **4.73%**; DII **21.32%**  
• Financials (CY26E): Revenue **Rs 10,535 cr**; Adj. PAT **Rs 1,088 cr**

Vidya Wires — Ventura Initiating Coverage

**Buy thesis: growth, deleveraging, rerating**

• Rating: **BUY** at CMP ~INR **74**  
• 24‑month TP: **INR 99** (~**35%** upside)  
• FY25–28 revenue CAGR: **~30.5%**  
• FY28E revenue: **~INR 3,303–3,380 cr**  
• FY28E PAT: **~INR 104 cr**  
• RoIC FY28E: **~34%** (high capital efficiency)  
• PEG: **~0.6x** — attractive growth‑valuation mix  
• Capacity doubling to **37,680 MTPA** by FY27  
• Promoter holding **~72.8%**, no pledge  

**Key catalysts / triggers**

• ALCU plant successful commissioning and ramp  
• Strong power & transmission capex recovery  
• Renewable and data‑center demand acceleration  
• Continued margin improvement and EBITDA/ton gains  
• Rapid net‑debt paydown to near **zero** by FY28  

**Primary risks / downside**

• Copper price spikes impair margins materially  
• Supplier concentration (top vendors ~**60%+**)  
• ALCU execution delays or cost overruns  
• High end‑market concentration: Power ~**48%**  
• Historically tight working capital; cash‑flow risk  

Investment view: high growth, rapid deleveraging, asymmetric upside.

**PAYTM – RBI ACTION & BROKERAGE VIEW ⚖️📉**

**RATING & TARGET**  
• Rating: Outperform  
• Target Price: ₹1,500  
• CMP: ₹1,147 → ~31% upside  

**KEY EVENT**  
• RBI cancels Paytm Payments Bank (PPBL) license (effective Apr 24, 2026)  
• PPBL to be wound up → no further banking operations  

**REGULATORY BACKDROP**  
• Ban on new customers since Mar 2022  
• Restrictions on deposits & wallet top-ups (Jan–Feb 2024)  
• Issues flagged: governance, compliance, depositor interest  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  

**NEGATIVE (SENTIMENTAL)**  
• Strong regulatory language remains a concern  
• Incremental negative from perception standpoint  

**NEUTRAL (FINANCIALS)**  
• PPBL already non-operational for ~1 year  
• Business separation already executed  
• Write-offs taken earlier → no fresh hit  

**POTENTIAL UPSIDE (OPTIONALITY)**  
• Scope to apply for:  
  • NBFC license  
  • PPI license  
• Enables:  
  • Wallet expansion  
  • Credit product scaling  

**FINANCIAL TRAJECTORY**  
• EPS: FY25 (₹-10.35) → FY26E ₹9.01 → FY27E ₹22.83  
• Revenue/share: steady growth trajectory  

**VALUATION SNAPSHOT**  
• P/E: 127x (FY26E) → 50x (FY27E)  
• High valuation but backed by earnings inflection  

**STOCK PERFORMANCE**  
• YTD: -11.7%  
• 1Y: +26.7%  
• Underperformance vs benchmark persists  

**CORE INSIGHT**  
• RBI action largely “known negative” → limited incremental financial impact  
• Business now cleaner, with regulatory overhang reducing over time  
• Key re-rating trigger = execution in credit + financial services  

**Impact:** Neutral to slightly positive — sentiment hit, but fundamentals unchanged 📊

**INFOSYS ADR – 7% DROP POST Q4 📉**

**WHY STREET IS CAUTIOUS**  

**WEAK GROWTH SIGNALS**  
• CC revenue: -1.3% QoQ  
• Below Street estimates  
• Weakest in 4 quarters  

**GUIDANCE DISAPPOINTMENT**  
• FY27 growth: 1.5–3.5%  
• Below est. 2–4%  
• Lower band below prior upgrade  

**EXECUTION RISKS**  
• March-end delays flagged  
• Headcount ↓ ~8,440 QoQ  
• Spillover risk into Q1  

**AI EXPECTATION GAP**  
• No fresh AI revenue update  
• Earlier ~5.5% disclosure  
• High expectations unmet  

**DEGROWTH TREND**  
• FY26 CC growth: ~3.1%  
• FY25: ~4.2%  
• Clear deceleration trend  

**CORE INSIGHT**  
• Growth visibility weakening  
• Guidance lacks confidence  
• Execution + AI clarity key  

**Impact:** Negative (near-term sentiment pressure)

Robust HVDC Pipeline in the Offing

According to the National Electricity Plan for Transmission, the CEA aims to scale the current HVDC operational capacity from 33.5 GW to 66.75 GW in FY32.

For this purpose,8-9 projects are being planned at a a total cost of ~ Rs 1,90,000 crore till FY32. Going beyond FY32, additional 6-7 projects are being planned with a Rs 1,00,000 crore investment.

HVDC theme is not a 1-2 year theme. Doesn't mean one should chase at any price. There will be intermittent corrections and that might be the opportunity for those who are not playing the theme.

Information credits: IDBI Capital
